worldSummary.title: World Summary
worldSummary.content: Etherealis is a brutal world shaped by six Primal Entities and bound together by the sentient Primal Weaves, where corrupted magic, ancient wars, and clashing civilizations fuel a realm of dark beauty and danger. Its lands are scarred by the First Corruption of the western continent, north frozen by the Frost King, South drowned by the Great Dreamer, and the giant walls of Aethelgard blocking all the corruption from the west, 10 kingdoms where races and factions rise, fall, and fight over elemental power, Weave mastery, and survival.

magicSystem.title: Magic System
magicSystem.content: The Primal Weave is a sentient cosmic magic brain that Weavers tap into using attunement to one or more Primal Entities. Six Primal Entities shaped the world from its Weave of reality: **Luminary Prime** (Artifice Weave), **Silent Oracle** (Mind Weave), **Great Forger** (Particle Weave), **Unbound** (Chrono Weave), **Flow Keeper** (Energy Weaving), and **Veiled Weaver** (Null Weaving). Corruption, collapse, or overuse of any Weave threatens both the caster and the realm’s balance.

subtypes.name: Artifice Weave
subtypes.description: From Luminary Prime, this magic creates illusions, deceptions, and dream infiltrations.

subtypes.name: Mind Weave
subtypes.description: From the Silent Oracle, this magic manipulates thought, fear, and memory.

subtypes.name: Particle Weave
subtypes.description: From the Great Forger, this magic controls elemental forces like fire, water, and earth.

subtypes.name: Chrono Weave
subtypes.description: From the Unbound, this magic manipulates time with freezes, fast-forwards, and spatial folds.

subtypes.name: Energy Weave
subtypes.description: From the Flow Keeper, this magic controls force, stamina, and power flow.

subtypes.name: Null Weave
subtypes.description: From the Veiled Weaver, this magic can heal, raise the dead, or open unstable dimensional rifts.

magicClasses.title: Magic Classes
magicClasses.content: Magic in Etherealis is diverse, manifesting in numerous specialized classes. These disciplines arise from combining different Primal Weaves, allowing casters to achieve a wide array of powerful effects, from manipulating the elements to bending time itself.

subtypes.name: Elemental
subtypes.description: This class involves the manipulation of earth, water, air, fire, etc. It primarily uses Particle Weaving and requires an understanding of the concept of matter states.

subtypes.name: Alchemy
subtypes.description: Focuses on the transmutation of substances and the creation of potions and compounds. This is achieved by combining Particle Weaving and Null Weaving, requiring knowledge of the concept of particles.

subtypes.name: Rune/Enchantment
subtypes.description: The practice of imbuing objects with magical properties. It draws upon Artifice Weaving, Particle Weaving, and Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Plant Alteration
subtypes.description: Involves affecting the growth, form, and properties of plants. This class uses Particle Weaving (nature) and Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Bio-Alteration
subtypes.description: Used for healing and manipulating biological structures. It combines Particle Weaving (biomass), Energy Weaving, and Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Mind Magic
subtypes.description: This discipline allows for telepathy and influencing the thoughts and emotions of others. It is purely derived from Mind Weaving.

subtypes.name: Dream Magic
subtypes.description: Allows a caster to enter, shape, or influence dreams. It is a product of combining Mind Weaving and Artifice Weaving (illusions).

subtypes.name: Illusion Magic
subtypes.description: Focuses on creating false sensory perceptions. This class stems from Artifice Weaving.

subtypes.name: Blood Magic
subtypes.description: A dark art derived from or affecting blood. It is considered a twisted form of Bio-Alteration, also using Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Aura (Energy) Magic
subtypes.description: The manipulation of life force or ambient energy for offense, defense, and other purposes. It is fueled by Energy Weaving.

subtypes.name: Shadow Magic
subtypes.description: Involves the manipulation of shadows and darkness. This class uses Particle Weaving (shadow) and potentially Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Time Magic
subtypes.description: A powerful discipline focused on the manipulation of the flow of time. It is derived from Chrono Weaving.

subtypes.name: Necromancy
subtypes.description: The grim art of raising and controlling the dead and manipulating life force. It primarily uses Null Weaving (life/death) and potentially Mind Weaving.

subtypes.name: Techno-Magic
subtypes.description: The practice of animating or enhancing machinery with magic. It combines Artifice Weaving and Energy Weaving.

subtypes.name: Space Magic
subtypes.description: Focuses on manipulating spatial dimensions and enabling teleportation. This class uses Chrono Weaving (space) and Null Weaving.

subtypes.name: Summoning/Taming
subtypes.description: Involves calling creatures or spirits and magically binding them. This is achieved through Null Weaving (dimensional) and Mind Weaving.

subtypes.name: Corruption Magic
subtypes.description: A malevolent class focused on inflicting negative magical effects and weakening foes. It uses Null Weaving (twisted energies) and potentially Shadow Magic.

subtypes.name: Positive Buff Magic
subtypes.description: Used for enhancing physical or magical abilities. This beneficial magic combines Energy Weaving and Null Weaving.

combatSystems.title: Technology
combatSystems.content: Etherealis blends magic and technology, creating powerful and often dangerous innovations. From city-powering Magitek Arms to time-altering Chrono-Stabilizers, technology is deeply integrated with the Primal Weaves. Factions and kingdoms leverage these advancements for warfare, industry, and survival.

subtypes.name: Magitek
subtypes.description: Magitek Arms power cities and armies with rune-etched cores. The Ironbrand Consortium develops magitek engines and sentinel armor, while Aethelgard uses Null Batteries to power its war engines.

subtypes.name: Weave-based Tech
subtypes.description: Arkhia uses Resonance Lenses to channel solar Weaves for industry. Chrono-Stabilizers can alter local time zones. Hover-Discs and Spellblades are common among elite mercenaries.

subtypes.name: Specialized Gear
subtypes.description: Vampire hunters from the Crimson Covenant and The Pale Order use specialized gear like blood-sensing gauntlets and silver-weave nets. Daring inventors also salvage and weaponize dangerous Remnant Tech.

races.title: Races
races.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"><path d="M17 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-4-4H5a4 4 0 0 0-4 4v2"></path><circle cx="9" cy="7" r="4"></circle><path d="M23 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-3-3.87"></path><path d="M16 3.13a4 4 0 0 1 0 7.75"></path></svg>
races.description: Etherealis is home to a diverse array of sentient races, each with unique cultures, abilities, and histories shaped by the world's brutal environment and the influence of the Primal Weaves.

subtypes.name: Humans
subtypes.description: Adaptable and diverse, humans have established kingdoms across Etherealis, from the frozen peaks of Eldrida to the sun-blasted dunes of Arkhia.

subRaces.name: Eldridian Humans (Frost-Walkers)
subRaces.description: From Eldrida, they endure icy blizzards and wield Particle and Energy Weaving over cold. They have pale skin, white or gray hair, and wear ivory masks during storms.

subRaces.name: Arkhian Humans
subRaces.description: From Arkhia, they thrive in sun-blasted desert dunes, controlling sunlight and air. They sport dark skin, sand-glass goggles, and vibrant robes.

subRaces.name: Imperial Humans
subRaces.description: From Aethelgard, they are culturally diverse, living in sprawling cities with caste systems, practicing all forms of magic with a focus on aesthetics and diplomacy.

subtypes.name: Elves
subtypes.description: An ancient and elegant race with a strong connection to magic, often divided by culture, habitat, and societal structures. Their history is marked by both great power and great tragedy.

subRaces.name: High Elves
subRaces.description: With ivory-gold skin and silver or golden hair, they reside in sunlit cities, dominating Artifice and Particle Weaving in a bloodline-obsessed society.

subRaces.name: Forest Elves
subRaces.description: Live in ancient groves, with earthy skin and nature-blending features. They wield earth magic and value harmony and clan loyalty.

subRaces.name: Water Elves
subRaces.description: Inhabit coastal and underwater cities, with aquatic hues and flowing garments. Many are enslaved or turned to piracy, mastering water magic.

subRaces.name: Snow Elves
subRaces.description: Born of frost, pale-skinned with red eyes, and adept in glacial magic. Near extinction, they suffer from vampirism and live in secluded tundras.

subRaces.name: Dark Elves
subRaces.description: Once of Zha’vren, they are shadowed and vengeful, with blue-dark skin, red or violet eyes, and high Null aptitude. Many are enslaved or part of the resistance.

subtypes.name: Terrans
subtypes.description: Enormous beings with bark-like skin, drawing strength from the earth. They value raw strength and natural balance.

subRaces.name: Emerald Clan
subRaces.description: Terrans with deep green, bark-like skin, closely connected to the forests and life.

subRaces.name: Bloodfist Clan
subRaces.description: Terrans with red, bark-like skin, known for their martial prowess and connection to the earth's raw power.

subtypes.name: Felkari
subtypes.description: Anthropomorphic feline races known for their agility, precision, and stealth.

subRaces.name: Neko
subRaces.description: Catlike beings, such as the Sunstripe and Tsuki clans, known for agility-based magic and precision.

subRaces.name: Pantherians
subRaces.description: Powerful stealth users of Null and Particle Weaves, like the Shadowclaw clan, revered for their lethal discipline.

subtypes.name: Ignar
subtypes.description: Born from fire and magma, their skin is traced with glowing flame-veins. They wield fire and light and value passion and duelling might.

subtypes.name: Demons
subtypes.description: Defy classification with shifting appearances and affinity for all Weaves. They command hidden realms and infect others through biotechnological marvels like vampirism.

subtypes.name: Sylphid
subtypes.description: Winged air-wielders with fair or dark skin, dwelling among sky-islands and embodying freedom and peace.

subtypes.name: Etherfolk
subtypes.description: Serene, psionic beings with lavender skin and immense mental discipline. They are isolated masters of Mind Weaving.

subtypes.name: Umbrin
subtypes.description: Mysterious black-skinned, silver-eyed Null Weavers from the Voidlands, deeply secretive and dimensionally aware.

subtypes.name: Remnants
subtypes.description: Conscious automata built by Demons, falling into three distinct castes based on their purpose and state of corruption.

subRaces.name: Forged Choir
subRaces.description: Loyal automatons created to serve their demonic masters without question.

subRaces.name: Keeper Class
subRaces.description: Unflinching dungeon guardians forged for eternal duty, often found guarding ancient sites.

subRaces.name: Echoed Blades
subRaces.description: Remnants that have been corrupted by chaotic Weave surges, making them unpredictable and dangerous.

entries.name: Frost Howler
entries.description: Horse-sized, ice-furred wolf with glacial fangs. It prowls Eldrida’s tundra, devours heat-energy, and its howls can trigger avalanches. Weak to overheating.

entries.name: Dune Serpent
entries.description: A 30 ft long serpentine beast with glass-like scales that burrows beneath Arkhia’s deserts. It ambushes prey, consumes minerals, and spews corrosive sand. Weak to water.

entries.name: Veil Stalker
entries.description: Humanoid creature with void-black skin that lurks in Null’s rifts. An ambush predator that drains life-force and can phase through matter. Burns in light.

entries.name: Rust-Eater
entries.description: Skeletal being with warped time-flesh that haunts ruined battlefields. It preys on the wounded, rapidly aging their injuries and turning victims into new cannibals.

entries.name: Leviathan Bonecruncher
entries.description: Colossal, coral-encrusted whale skeleton that lurks in abyssal trenches. It manipulates whirlpools and illusions, feeding on shipwrecks.

entries.name: Ember Ghoul
entries.description: Molten humanoids born in Ignaros volcanoes. They have an explosive touch, steal energy, and are weak to ice.

entries.name: The Great Dreamer
entries.description: Mountain-sized, humanoid-squid hybrid with bioluminescent coral tentacles that hypnotize and crush fleets. It is worshipped by cultists.

entries.name: Void King
entries.description: A mountain-sized, cosmic dragon with black scales that absorb everything, devouring magic. It is resisted only by Chrono paradoxes or core-shattering attacks.

entries.name: Eldrida
entries.description: Ruled by Jarl Borin Ice-Heart, this kingdom is set in glacial peaks, housing Eldridian Humans. Known for ice berserkers, a Nordic lifestyle, and geothermal forges using Particle and Energy weaves.

importantPlaces.name: The Frostheart Forge
importantPlaces.description: Buried beneath Eldrida’s peak, this forge is powered by glacial bloodfire and used by rune-smiths to shape ancient weapons.

entries.name: Arkhia
entries.description: Ruled by Sultan Zafira Sun-Speaker, this kingdom spreads across shifting sands. Home to wind-singers and resonance-glass technology powered by solar energy and Particle Weaving.

importantPlaces.name: The Sunscar Spire
importantPlaces.description: A massive spire that channels pure solar Weave into skyforged glass and resonance technology, powering much of the kingdom.

entries.name: Thalassir
entries.description: Ruled by Tide-King Orin Wavecrown, a large coastal city of Water Elves, surviving dark elves, and humans. A gigantic coral tree illuminates the city at night. Features coral marines and Hydro/Biolum Lumin power.

importantPlaces.name: The Leviathan Trenches
importantPlaces.description: Deep abyssal trenches below Thalassir, swirling with drowned ruins, sea-phantoms, and forbidden siren temples.

entries.name: Ignaros
entries.description: Ruled by Forge-Queen Ember, this kingdom erupts with Ignar magma forges and Ember Ghoul armies, driven by volcanic magic.

importantPlaces.name: The Ember Crucible
importantPlaces.description: Houses everburning magma fonts and volcanic heartshards that are mined for warcraft and powerful enchantments.

entries.name: Aethelgard
entries.description: Controlled by Grand Chancellor Silas Vance, this kingdom builds towering spires and magitek academies. It enforces Elven slavery and is protected by the massive Aegis Wall.

importantPlaces.name: The Aegis Wall
importantPlaces.description: A 300-foot magisteel barrier pulsing with Null wards, defending Aethelgard from demon breaches.

importantPlaces.name: Sentinel Academy
importantPlaces.description: A fortress-college built into the Aegis Wall, training elite Weave warriors through brutal trials and memory binds.

entries.name: Zha’vren
entries.description: Once the powerful kingdom of the Dark Elves, it now lies in ruin after being conquered by Aethelgard. It is the birthplace of the Shadowveil Resistance.

importantPlaces.name: The Ruins of Zha'vren
importantPlaces.description: The shattered remains of the former Dark Elf capital, now a contested zone and a symbol of their lost glory and enduring defiance.

factions.title: Factions
factions.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-users"><path d="M16 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-4-4H6a4 4 0 0 0-4 4v2"/><circle cx="9" cy="7" r="4"/><path d="M22 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-3-3.87"/><path d="M16 3.13a4 4 0 0 1 0 7.75"/></svg>

entries.name: Nullguard
entries.description: Led by Malakar Void-Sighted, these fanatics use corrupted Remnant tech to seal rifts and purge demons.

entries.name: Shadowveil Resistance
entries.description: Led by Veyra Duskspear, this faction fights to liberate enslaved Elves through underground rebellion and mastery of Null Weaving.

entries.name: Sentinel Academy
entries.description: Commanded by Rael, this academy trains morally grey multi-weave warriors to guard the Aegis Wall against demonic threats.

entries.name: Lumina Sanctum
entries.description: Ruled by High Lord Celestis, this faction monopolizes Artifice magic and upholds the elitist rule of High Elves, supporting slavery.

entries.name: Crimson Covenant
entries.description: A vampire-hunting brotherhood led by Grand Marshal Lior Ravenbrand, they wield enchanted weapons and track infected hosts across the land.

entries.name: Ashen Syndicate
entries.description: Led by the Furnace King, this syndicate weaponizes volcanic Weaves and Ember Ghouls for power, controlling the cures for the Weave-Sickness they profit from.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-history"><path d="M3 3v5h5"/><path d="M3.05 13A9 9 0 1 0 12 3v5"/><path d="M12 7v6h3"/></svg>

entries.description: The Primal Sundering: A cosmic event that fractures the cosmos and leads to the creation of mortals.
First Corruption: Demons emerge from a Null rift, slaughtering the Etherfolk. This leads to the Veiled Weaver's binding and the founding of the Nullguard.
The Aegis Wall is Forged: 10,000 mages sacrifice themselves to forge the magisteel Aegis Wall, ending the first Demon Wars and leading to the launch of Sentinel Academy.
The Fall of Zha’vren: Aethelgard destroys the Dark Elf kingdom and enslaves its people, giving rise to the Shadowveil Resistance.
Legalization of Slavery: Aethelgard, with the support of the Lumina Sanctum, legalizes the enslavement of Forest and Dark Elves, while sparing High Elves.
The Sentinel Betrayal: The Aegis Wall is sabotaged from within, unleashing a new crisis and a new wave of demonic threats upon the world.

outro.title: The Weave Beckons
outro.description: The story of Etherealis is ever-evolving, written in the clash of kingdoms and the whispers of the Weave. Your own legend awaits. Thank you for exploring this world.
